perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . ZipRealty and Company agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except the Permitted Purpose. Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who need to have the information in order to carry out the Permitted Purpose. Each party has had or will have each of its Representatives who has access to Confidential Information of the other party agree to maintain the confidentiality thereof in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. In any event, each party will be liable for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or nonpermitted use of Confidential Information of the other party in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have any such information, which measures will include the highest degree of care that either party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . ZipRealty and Company agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except the Permitted Purpose, unless prior written consent is obtained from the disclosing party's legal counsel for a different purpose. Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who need to have the information in order to carry out the Permitted Purpose. Each party has had or will have each of its Representatives who has access to Confidential Information of the other party agree to maintain the confidentiality thereof in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. In any event, each party will only be liable for intentional breaches of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or nonpermitted use of Confidential Information of the other party in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have any such information. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces several in-text contradictions. It allows for use of confidential information beyond the Permitted Purpose with legal counsel's consent, conflicting with the initial strict limitation. It changes liability for breaches by Representatives to only intentional breaches, contradicting the original strict liability. Also, it changes the measures from 'highest degree of care' to 'commercially reasonable measures', creating ambiguity and conflicting with the intended rigor of protecting Confidential Information. These changes undermine the original, stricter provisions of the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . ZipRealty and Company agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except the Permitted Purpose, unless prior written consent is obtained from the disclosing party's legal counsel for a different purpose. Neither party will disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who need to have the information in order to carry out the Permitted Purpose. Each party has had or will have each of its Representatives who has access to Confidential Information of the other party agree to maintain the confidentiality thereof in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. In any event, each party will only be liable for intentional breaches of this Agreement by any of its Representatives. Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or nonpermitted use of Confidential Information of the other party in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have any such information. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several in-text contradictions as described in the perturbation explanation. The original text had a strict limitation on using confidential information, only allowing it for the Permitted Purpose. The changed text modifies this by allowing for use of confidential information beyond the Permitted Purpose with legal counsel's consent. The original text stated that each party would be liable for breaches by its Representatives but the Changed Text modifies the liability for breaches by Representatives to only intentional breaches. Also, the original text states measures will include the 'highest degree of care' which is then changed to 'commercially reasonable measures'. The Contradicted Text is the Changed Text, meaning that these changes directly contradict the initial strict provisions of the non-disclosure agreement within the same section, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Materials . Any materials or documents that have been furnished by or on behalf of the disclosing party to the receiving party or its Representatives will, promptly upon the disclosing party’s request, at the receiving party’s option, either (i) be returned to the disclosing party, and all copies of such documentation will be destroyed, or (ii) be destroyed (along with all copies of such documentation), such destruction to be certified in writing to the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Materials . Any materials or documents that have been furnished by or on behalf of the disclosing party to the receiving party or its Representatives will, upon the disclosing party’s request, be returned to the disclosing party. The receiving party can retain one archival copy for legal compliance purposes. Destruction of copies is at the receiving party's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating the receiving party 'will' return materials and removes the 'option' to destroy them. Furthermore, it allows the receiving party to keep one archival copy, contradicting the original requirement of destroying all copies or returning them. This creates uncertainty about the receiving party's obligations regarding material return and destruction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return of Materials . Any materials or documents that have been furnished by or on behalf of the disclosing party to the receiving party or its Representatives will, upon the disclosing party’s request, be returned to the disclosing party. The receiving party can retain one archival copy for legal compliance purposes. Destruction of copies is at the receiving party's discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the original text. The original text provided the receiving party with the 'option' to either return or destroy the materials. The changed text removes this option, requiring that the materials 'will' be returned. Furthermore, the changed text explicitly allows the receiving party to retain one archival copy, which is contradictory to the implied obligation in the original text to destroy all copies if they opt to destroy instead of return. Since the contradicted text is the changed text itself, this makes the contradiction clear.

perturbation.original_text: 7. No License Granted . Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party or any of its Representatives under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right, nor does this Agreement grant either party or any of its Representatives any rights in or to the other party’s information, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. No License Granted . Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party or any of its Representatives under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right, nor does this Agreement grant either party or any of its Representatives any rights in or to the other party’s information, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Permitted Purpose. However, either party may, at its discretion, grant a limited, non-exclusive license to the other party for specific intellectual property rights related to the Permitted Purpose, subject to a separate written agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by allowing either party to grant a 'limited, non-exclusive license' for intellectual property rights, which contradicts the initial statement that 'Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights...under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right'. This introduces ambiguity and the potential for conflict regarding intellectual property usage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. No License Granted . Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to either party or any of its Representatives under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right, nor does this Agreement grant either party or any of its Representatives any rights in or to the other party’s information, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Permitted Purpose. However, either party may, at its discretion, grant a limited, non-exclusive license to the other party for specific intellectual property rights related to the Permitted Purpose, subject to a separate written agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights...under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other intellectual property right'. The changed text then introduces a clause allowing either party to grant a 'limited, non-exclusive license' for intellectual property rights, subject to a separate written agreement. This directly contradicts the original statement, as it now allows for the granting of rights that were initially prohibited, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text and changed text are identical, reinforcing the fact that the change causes a contradiction within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Term . The foregoing commitments in this Agreement will survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and will continue for a period of two (2) years following the date of this Agreement, except to the extent explicitly terminated in writing by both ZipRealty and Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Term . The foregoing commitments in this Agreement will survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and will continue for a period of one (1) year following the date of this Agreement, but may be extended automatically for additional one-year terms unless either party provides written notice of termination 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term. Any termination must be explicitly stated in writing by both ZipRealty and Company to be effective prior to the end of the initial term.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction. It initially shortens the term from two years to one, but then allows for automatic yearly extensions unless terminated. It further contradicts the original termination clause by requiring joint written agreement for early termination, while also allowing for unilateral termination at the end of each extended term. This creates confusion about the agreement's duration and termination conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. Term . The foregoing commitments in this Agreement will survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and will continue for a period of one (1) year following the date of this Agreement, but may be extended automatically for additional one-year terms unless either party provides written notice of termination 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term. Any termination must be explicitly stated in writing by both ZipRealty and Company to be effective prior to the end of the initial term.
perturbation.justification: The modified text shortens the initial term to one year and introduces the possibility of automatic yearly extensions, which contradicts the original two-year term. Furthermore, it introduces a new unilateral termination clause (30 days notice), which conflicts with the initial requirement of explicit written termination by both parties for early termination. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, demonstrating an in-text contradiction because the same section now contains conflicting information about the term length and termination conditions.
